title: Understanding Power
text: Power is the rate at which work is done or energy is transferred. In rotational motion, power can be calculated using the formula: Power $$=$$ Torque * Angular Velocity.

title: Angular Velocity Conversion
text: The angular velocity is given in revolutions per minute $$\frac{rev}{min}$$. Convert this to radians per second $$\frac{rad}{s}$$ using the conversion factor: $$1$$ rev $$=$$ $$2\pi$$ rad and $$1$$ min $$=$$ $$60$$ s.

title: Calculate Angular Velocity at $$3.0$$ s
text: First, find the angular velocity at $$3.0$$ seconds. Use the formula: Angular Velocity $$=$$ Initial Angular Velocity + (Angular Acceleration * Time).

title: Calculate Angular Acceleration
text: Angular acceleration can be found using the formula: Angular Acceleration $$=$$ (Final Angular Velocity - Initial Angular Velocity) / Time.

title: Calculate Power at $$3.0$$ s
text: Now, calculate the power at $$3.0$$ seconds using the formula: Power $$=$$ Torque * Angular Velocity.
